ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-04-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Trustee and Conservator).

Ms. Alavi stated that the exchange traded funds (ETF's) were orginally approved on 9/26/14, that they are appropriate and produce income, that there is a little over $21,000.00 in gains and a small loss, that without the ETF's they wouldn't produce income, and that it wasn't reapproved. Ms. Alavi also provided the allocation breakdown, addressed the Trustee's fees at 1% noting CRC 7.776, stated that her declaration filed on 1/16/19 addresses the fees, that 1% is customary/standard, that the amount is far less than what banks charge, that the petition was served on all parties, and that no objections were filed. Ms. Alavi further stated that Mr. Hodges brings 25 years of experience, that he bears all the responsibilities, that his services include dealing with the farmland in North Dakota, that allowing fees at 1% assists Mr. Hodges in self-insuring, that professionals must even use their own money to defend unwarranted claims, that there are significant overhead costs, and that there is also the assumption/risk. In closing, Ms. Alavi addressed the four missing CalPers payments stating that the November 2016 and June 2017 missing payments have been received. At the conclusion of Ms. Alavi's comments, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.

Petition For Authority To Invest Funds (Probate Code §2570)
Off Calendar Sep-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Siracusa explained that the 2.1 estimated income of the portfolio does not take into account the potential growth which is anticipated to be 5 to 7% on top of that income after deducting Merrill Lynch's fees. Mr. Siracusa also stated that they could change the investment percentages to 25% in equities, 50% in bonds and 25% in cash if needed. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Invest Funds (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
